Package com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components

Class NewTrade

java.lang.Object
com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components.NewTrade
public class NewTrade extends Object
NewTrade - The new trade that is booked.

    accountId()
    A globally unique identifier referencing a single account.
    Optional<String>

    The current activity_id of this trade in the Ledger.

    additionalInstructions()
    Free form instructions that can be used to provide additional instructions (that are not captured by existing special instructions) and will be put on the trade confirm.

    Fractional support for market-makers' internal order ids.

  • Constructor Details

    • NewTrade

      public NewTrade(Optional<String> accountId, Optional<String> activityId, Optional<String> additionalInstructions, Optional<String> alternateOrderId, Optional<? extends RebookTradeResponseAssetType> assetType, Optional<? extends RebookTradeResponseBrokerCapacity> brokerCapacity, Optional<String> clientOrderId, Optional<String> executingBroker, Optional<? extends List<Execution>> executions, Optional<? extends List<BookingFee>> fees, Optional<String> identifier, Optional<? extends RebookTradeResponseIdentifierType> identifierType, Optional<String> issuingRegionCode, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends RebookTradeResponseLocalMarketTradeDate> localMarketTradeDate, Optional<? extends List<BookingLot>> lotMatchingInstructions, Optional<String> micCode, Optional<String> name, Optional<Boolean> open, Optional<String> orderId, Optional<? extends RebookTradeResponseRouteType> routeType, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends RebookTradeResponseSettlementDate> settlementDate, Optional<? extends RebookTradeResponseSide> side, Optional<? extends RebookTradeResponseSideModifier> sideModifier, Optional<String> sourceApplication, Optional<? extends List<RebookTradeResponseSpecialInstructions>> specialInstructions, Optional<String> tradeId, Optional<? extends RebookTradeResponseVenue> venue, Optional<? extends RebookTradeResponseWhenIssued> whenIssued)

    • NewTrade

      public NewTrade()

  • Method Details

    • accountId

      public Optional<String> accountId()
      A globally unique identifier referencing a single account.

    • activityId

      public Optional<String> activityId()
      The current activity_id of this trade in the Ledger.

    • additionalInstructions

      public Optional<String> additionalInstructions()
      Free form instructions that can be used to provide additional instructions (that are not captured by existing special instructions) and will be put on the trade confirm.

    • alternateOrderId

      public Optional<String> alternateOrderId()
      Fractional support for market-makers' internal order ids.

    • assetType

      public Optional<RebookTradeResponseAssetType> assetType()
      Type of the asset being traded. Required for SYMBOL and CUSIP.

    • brokerCapacity

      public Optional<RebookTradeResponseBrokerCapacity> brokerCapacity()
      Broker capacity for the trade.

    • clientOrderId

      public Optional<String> clientOrderId()
      The unique identifier that is associated with an order. Must be unique by date per trade per client.

    • executingBroker

      public Optional<String> executingBroker()
      Executing broker of the trade.

    • executions

      public Optional<List<Execution>> executions()
      The executions (sometimes referred to as partial-fills) that comprise the trade.

    • fees

      public Optional<List<BookingFee>> fees()
      Any client calculated fees associated with the trade. Only allowed if trade.open = false. Regulatory fees will be calculated automatically if they are not explicitly overwritten or suppressed.

    • identifier

      public Optional<String> identifier()
      Identifier (of the type specified in `identifier_type`). Responses will supply the originally requested identifier.

    • identifierType

      public Optional<RebookTradeResponseIdentifierType> identifierType()
      Identifier type for the asset being traded.

    • issuingRegionCode

      public Optional<String> issuingRegionCode()
      Unicode CLDR region code. Issuing Region Code is required for some `identifier_type`s, especially CUSIP.

    • localMarketTradeDate

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<RebookTradeResponseLocalMarketTradeDate> localMarketTradeDate()
      Date field to support extended trading hours.

    • lotMatchingInstructions

      public Optional<List<BookingLot>> lotMatchingInstructions()
      One or many lot matching instructions for the trade.

    • micCode

      public Optional<String> micCode()
      Market Identifier Code

    • name

      public Optional<String> name()
      The resource name of the trade.

    • open

      public Optional<Boolean> open()
      State of this trade's completeness in filling. True: trade is not done filling and can append more executions onto the trade False: trade is done filling and cannot append more executions onto the trade By default, trades are closed when they are created. An open trade can later be closed by calling the CompleteTrade endpoint. Additional executions can be appended to an open trade by calling the CreateExecution endpoint. Trades that are left open will be automatically closed nightly before Ledger's EOD.

    • orderId

      public Optional<String> orderId()
      Street-level order id, unique by day per broker.

    • routeType

      public Optional<RebookTradeResponseRouteType> routeType()
      Route type for the trade.

    • settlementDate

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<RebookTradeResponseSettlementDate> settlementDate()
      Defaults to T+1 for equities if this is not provided. Calculated by the execution's execution_time field in Eastern Time.

    • side

      public Optional<RebookTradeResponseSide> side()
      Denotes if the trade is a SELL or a BUY.

    • sideModifier

      public Optional<RebookTradeResponseSideModifier> sideModifier()
      Side modifier for the trade.

    • sourceApplication

      public Optional<String> sourceApplication()
      The source of the submission.

    • specialInstructions

      public Optional<List<RebookTradeResponseSpecialInstructions>> specialInstructions()
      An enumerated list of values used to indicate certain attributes about a trade (E.g. DISCRETION_EXERCISED, BROKER_LIQUIDATION) and/or trigger downstream processing rules (e.g. SUPPRESS_TRACE_REPORTING)

    • tradeId

      public Optional<String> tradeId()
      A ULID to uniquely identify the trade globally.

    • venue

      public Optional<RebookTradeResponseVenue> venue()
      Exchange venue

    • whenIssued

      public Optional<RebookTradeResponseWhenIssued> whenIssued()
      Denotes that this trade was either when_issued or when_distributed.

    • builder

      public static final NewTrade.Builder builder()

      A globally unique identifier referencing a single account.

      Any client calculated fees associated with the trade. Only allowed if trade.open = false. Regulatory fees will be calculated automatically if they are not explicitly overwritten or suppressed.

    • withOpen

      public NewTrade withOpen(boolean open)
      State of this trade's completeness in filling. True: trade is not done filling and can append more executions onto the trade False: trade is done filling and cannot append more executions onto the trade By default, trades are closed when they are created. An open trade can later be closed by calling the CompleteTrade endpoint. Additional executions can be appended to an open trade by calling the CreateExecution endpoint. Trades that are left open will be automatically closed nightly before Ledger's EOD.

    • withOpen

      public NewTrade withOpen(Optional<Boolean> open)
      State of this trade's completeness in filling. True: trade is not done filling and can append more executions onto the trade False: trade is done filling and cannot append more executions onto the trade By default, trades are closed when they are created. An open trade can later be closed by calling the CompleteTrade endpoint. Additional executions can be appended to an open trade by calling the CreateExecution endpoint. Trades that are left open will be automatically closed nightly before Ledger's EOD.

      An enumerated list of values used to indicate certain attributes about a trade (E.g. DISCRETION_EXERCISED, BROKER_LIQUIDATION) and/or trigger downstream processing rules (e.g. SUPPRESS_TRACE_REPORTING)

    • withTradeId

      public NewTrade withTradeId(String tradeId)
      A ULID to uniquely identify the trade globally.

    • withTradeId

      public NewTrade withTradeId(Optional<String> tradeId)
      A ULID to uniquely identify the trade globally.

    • withVenue

      public NewTrade withVenue(RebookTradeResponseVenue venue)
      Exchange venue

    • withVenue

      public NewTrade withVenue(Optional<? extends RebookTradeResponseVenue> venue)
      Exchange venue

    • withWhenIssued

      public NewTrade withWhenIssued(RebookTradeResponseWhenIssued whenIssued)
      Denotes that this trade was either when_issued or when_distributed.

    • withWhenIssued

      public NewTrade withWhenIssued(Optional<? extends RebookTradeResponseWhenIssued> whenIssued)
      Denotes that this trade was either when_issued or when_distributed.
